article_items.title: ZeroDrift raises $10M to protect AI models from themselves | TechCrunch
article_items.description: A new AI compliance service sits between AI models and end users to flag and replace any messages that might present a compliance problem.
article_items.excerpt: A new AI compliance service sits between AI models and end users to flag and replace any messages that might present a compliance problem.
article_items.summary: That’s the premise of ZeroDrift , a new AI compliance service that on Tuesday said it had raised $10 million in a seed funding round that saw investments from a16z Speedrun, Reign Ventures, PitchDrive Ventures, and U&I Ventures, among others. More from TechCrunch ZeroDrift raises $10M to protect AI models from themselves As enterprises troubleshoot their AI systems, governance has emerged as a key challenge. The company deals entirely with the second part of the system, sitting between AI models and end users to flag and replace any messages that might present a compliance problem. Critically, the company says its entire system can be run with lower latency and more reliability than a conventional LLM. The first StrictlyVC of 2026 hits SF on April 30.

article_items.title: Rocket engine startup Impulse raises $500 million to hire people, not AI | TechCrunch
article_items.description: Engineering physical systems still depends on human talent, according to Impulse Space president Eric Romo.
article_items.excerpt: Engineering physical systems still depends on human talent, according to Impulse Space president Eric Romo.
article_items.summary: Romo said the company is prepping a new Mira mission that is expected to launch before the end of the year. Next up for the company is another launch of its Mira spacecraft, which made its third flight late last year. While the company’s software teams are adopting AI coding tools, Romo said that when it comes to solving engineering problems in the real world, deep learning models aren’t quite ready for prime time. As the 13th employee at SpaceX back in 2003, Romo’s job was creating computer simulations of the company’s engine design to assess its performance. Since its launch, the Alpamayo open platform has seen rapid adoption across both industry and academia, with Alpamayo reasoning models collectively surpassing 400,000 downloads to date.

Introduced at CES 2026 and expanded at GTC 2026 , the Alpamayo open platform provides researchers and developers with a flexible, high-performance, and scalable suite of models, datasets, simulation, and training tools.

Alpamayo 2 Super is built on the Cosmos 3 Super Reasoner 32-billion parameter VLM backbone (3x the number of parameters as prior Alpamayo models), is RL post-trained, and introduces support for surround-view camera.
Based in part on feedback from the community, we are introducing several new additions to the Alpamayo open platform across models and training infrastructure.

Key Highlights of this New Release Fully-fledged RL training infrastructure: AlpaGym launches with all the tooling and components necessary to get started with closed-loop reinforcement learning immediately.

Key Highlights of this New Release 3x parameter scale: Alpamayo 2 Super scales to 32B parameters (compared to previous 10B-parameter generations), improving reasoning, 3D spatial understanding and trajectory prediction.
Reasoning models in AVs will unlock new capabilities and levels of safety for the next generation of autonomous systems.
Intelligent reactive agents: We are releasing a learning-based traffic model (based on CAT-K ) that enables simulated agents to react and respond to the ego-vehicle’s actions in closed-loop.

article_items.summary: Computer Use Across GUI Environments and Agent Harnesses Based on the Qwen family, Holo3.1 was designed to improve robustness across the environments where computer-use agents are actually deployed, while retaining state-of-the-art performance. For the first time, we release quantized checkpoints optimized for local inference, including FP8, Q4 GGUF, and NVFP4. A Blog post by H company on Hugging Face Holo3.1: Fast & Local Computer Use Agents Users want to run the same computer-use capabilities across desktop and mobile environments, with seamless integration with different agent frameworks. On AndroidWorld, our 35B-A3B model improves from 67% to 79.3%, while the smaller 4B and 9B variants improve from 58% to 72%. Across OSWorld and our internal benchmark suite covering e-commerce, business software, and collaboration workflows, function-calling and native execution now achieve near-parity performance. article_items.title: How Baz improved its AI Agent Code Review accuracy using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore | Amazon Web Services
article_items.description: This post walks through how Baz built their Spec Review agent using Amazon Bedrock and Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. We'll cover the architecture decisions, implementation details, and the business outcomes they achieved by leveraging these AWS services to automate their code review process
article_items.excerpt: This post walks through how Baz built their Spec Review agent using Amazon Bedrock and Amazon Bedrock AgentCore. We'll cover the architecture decisions, implementation details, and the business outcomes they achieved by leveraging these AWS services to automate their code review process
article_items.summary: Enabling intelligent code review with Amazon Bedrock Amazon Bedrock powers the reasoning and decision-making layer behind the Spec Reviewer agent, enabling it to interpret requirements, understand design intent, and evaluate the relevance of behaviors observed in the browser. Amazon Bedrock AgentCore isolation, sandboxing, and observability help Baz scale multiple MCP servers and allow the agent to safely and reliably perform full-stack validation at scale. Nimrod Kor is the Co-Founder and CTO of Baz, where he leads the company’s engineering and AI architecture efforts focused on transforming how developers review and ship code. Its secure, isolated, serverless browser sessions allow the Spec Reviewer agent to open preview environments, navigate through features, and examine UI behavior exactly as a user would. Despite the uncertainty about the future of work, one thing is for sure: change is coming, and in some cases, it is already here. “The fundamental nature of work is changing,” said Nikhil Krishnan, CTO of AI software platform vendor C3 AI , on the Targeting AI podcast from AI Business . “We're on the cusp of probably the next industrial revolution and how it evolves,” he said.

Navigating the Future of Work with AI The future of work is marked by roles in which AI agents perform low-risk tasks autonomously, while also involving some human collaboration.

He was previously a senior news and features writer covering health IT and HR software at TechTarget and a senior news director overseeing coverage of AI, business analytics, data management and government tech.
Despite the uncertainty about the future of work, one thing is for sure: change is coming, and in some cases, it is already here.

With AI agents and generative AI continuing to affect every aspect of work, there remains a conflicting narrative about what the future of work looks like as technology advances: whether AI replaces workers or augments.

Related: SoftBank Commits $87.3B to France AI Infrastructure Buildout A Paradox of Responsibility However, while Anthropic has maintained its reputation as a responsible AI company, the IPO filing brings new pressure. If Anthropic’s IPO is successful and the vendor becomes a publicly traded company, it can’t switch its mode of operation and suddenly become focused solely on maximizing shareholder value, Bennett said. article_items.why_matters: Related: SoftBank Commits $87.3B to France AI Infrastructure Buildout A Paradox of Responsibility However, while Anthropic has maintained its reputation as a responsible AI company, the IPO filing brings new pressure. If Anthropic’s IPO is successful and the vendor becomes a publicly traded company, it can’t switch its mode of operation and suddenly become focused solely on maximizing shareholder value, Bennett said.
article_items.what_to_watch: Even though OpenAI models helped bring wide adoption to generative AI, Anthropic focused on the enterprise market by making Claude the premium enterprise coding model.
key_points: “The company seems to be building a really powerful moat around itself,” Bennett said, referring to model performance and moral responsibility in deploying the model.
Even though OpenAI models helped bring wide adoption to generative AI, Anthropic focused on the enterprise market by making Claude the premium enterprise coding model.
When she's not diving deep into the world of AI, she spends her time on passion projects and raising her three daughters.

“Everything of late indicates that Anthropic is well positioned to maintain its current situation, with respect to the others as the most widely recognized … most powerful model,” said Michael G Bennet, associate vice.

“The most widely recognized as being significantly more advanced than the others.” While OpenAI GPT models are competitive with Anthropic Claude models, Anthropic has spent the last few years solidifying its reputation.
The filing comes after the Claude creator revealed on May 28 that its valuation had shot up to $965 billion following a $65 billion Series H funding round, surpassing rival OpenAI's $852 valuation.
While the fight against the administration could have eroded its market share, Anthropic showed enterprises and consumers that the AI lab could be trusted to deliver responsible AI.
This trust was magnified to some when Anthropic co-founder Christopher Olah met with Pope Leo XIV at the Vatican on May 25 and, together with the Pope, called for global moral oversight of AI.
The Commitment to Responsible AI Anthropic went head-to-head with the Trump administration earlier this year by refusing to let its models be used for some military activities.

Related: SoftBank Commits $87.3B to France AI Infrastructure Buildout A Paradox of Responsibility However, while Anthropic has maintained its reputation as a responsible AI company, the IPO filing brings new pressure.

Anthropic’s IPO Filing and How It Affects Its Responsible AI Stance Anthropic’s IPO Filing and How It Affects Its Responsible AI Stance The IPO reflects the vendor’s accomplishments, even with its responsible AI stance.
The move underscores the generative AI vendor’s success but also creates tension around its ethical commitments.

rag_rules: Make the business move concrete: who is involved, what changed, and why now.
Tie funding, partnerships, or revenue to product direction or competitive pressure.
Avoid hype words unless the article gives evidence.
Lead with the actual product, model, or API name.
